dhp76:1: Regard him as one who points out treasure,
dhp76:2: the wise one who seeing your faults
dhp76:4: Stay with this sort of sage.
dhp76:5: For the one who stays with a sage of this sort,
dhp77:2: deflect you away from poor manners.
dhp79:1: Drinking the Dhamma, refreshed by the Dhamma, one sleeps at ease
dhp79:3: In the Dhamma revealed by the noble ones,
dhp81:1: As a single slab of rock
dhp81:3: so the wise are not moved
dhp82:3: so the wise become clear, calm,
dhp82:4: on hearing words of the Dhamma.
dhp83:1: Everywhere, truly, those of integrity stand apart.
dhp83:2: They, the good, don’t chatter in hopes of favor or gains.
dhp83:3: When touched now by pleasure, now pain,
dhp83:4: the wise give no sign of high or low.
dhp84:1: One who wouldn’t– not for his own sake nor that of another–
dhp84:2: hanker for wealth, a son, a kingdom,
dhp84:3: his own fulfillment, by unrighteous means:
dhp84:4: he is righteous, rich in virtue, discernment.
dhp86:1: But those who practice Dhamma in line with the well-taught Dhamma,
dhp86:3: will cross over the realm of Death
dhp87:2: the wise person should develop the bright,
dhp87:3: having gone from home to no-home
dhp87:4: in seclusion, so hard to enjoy.
dhp88:1: There he should wish for delight,
dhp88:2: discarding sensuality– he who has nothing.
dhp89:6: they, in the world, are Unbound.
